name: INR/USD Exchange Rate
summary: Day 71 Saturday (today markets closed). **INR ₹94.47/USD (-0.1% 1D MAY 8) — STABILIZED IN ₹94.4-94.7 RANGE; ₹95 TRIGGER STILL REVERSED.** Brent close $101.73 (+1.66% 1D) is materially below pre-May-4 baseline but still well above pre-crisis ($73), keeping CAD pressure persistent. DXY 97.84 (-0.4% 1D, -1.0% 1M); EUR/USD 1.18 (+0.4% 1D); USD/CNY 6.80 (-0.2% 1D); broader EM/G10 USD weakness regime continues. **CONTRADICTION:** USD/KRW ₩1,461.48 (+1.2% 1D) — won weakening despite KOSPI strength = foreign capital flowing INTO Korea while broader EM macro mixed. India is taking it: Nifty -0.6%, Sensex -0.7%, Bank Nifty -1.3% on May 8; FII outflows ₹340.89 cr May 8; ₹1.92 LAKH CR YTD 2026 already exceeds full-year 2025 (₹1.66 lakh cr); India 10Y G-Sec ~7.0%, +6-8 bps cycle. RBI capital-flow curbs (NOP cap $100mn/day + INR NDF restriction) operational. FX reserves >$700B. ₹95.50 direct-intervention threshold UNTESTED. **Iranian rial DEPRECIATING: 1,812,000 IRR/USD remittance May 8 vs 1,775,000 May 7** — yesterday's 'first MOU-related rial appreciation' chirp silenced; rial down ~2.1% over two sessions. Decision-relevant binary: if MOU signs by May 14, 5-15% IRR appreciation prices in within 7-14 days; INR continued mild appreciation toward ₹93-94 range. If Iran rejects past May 21, INR weakens back toward ₹95-96 record territory; rial accelerates depreciation toward 2M Brookings hyperinflation trigger.
